chunks.text: Pieter Abbeel is a Professor at UC Berkeley EECS, located in 746 Sutardja Dai Hall, with contact email pabbeel@cs.berkeley.edu and phone (510) 642-7034. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR). He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2008 and an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from KU Leuven, Belgium in 2000. Office hours are arranged via email.

Elad Alon is an Adjunct Professor at UC Berkeley EECS, located in 519 Cory Hall, with contact email elad@eecs.berkeley.edu and phone 510-642-0237. His research interests include Integrated Circuits (INC), Design, Modeling and Analysis (DMA), Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), and Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC). He earned his Ph.D., M.S., and B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University in 2006, 2002, and 2001 respectively. Office hours are on Tuesday from 2:30-3:30pm in 519 Cory.

Venkat Anantharam is a Professor at UC Berkeley EECS, located in 271 Cory Hall, with contact email ananth@eecs.berkeley.edu and phone 510-643-8435. His research interests include Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), Artificial Intelligence (AI), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Security (SEC), and Signal Processing (SP). He earned his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from UC Berkeley in 1986, along with degrees in Mathematics from UC Berkeley. Office hours vary by week, and appointments can be made via email, with assistant Kim Kail available at 253 Cory Hall.

Gopala Krishna Anumanchipalli is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley EECS, located in 490A Cory Hall, with contact email gopala@berkeley.edu. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Signal Processing (SP), and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). He earned his Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Instituto Superior Técnico, Lisbon, Portugal in 2013 and another Ph.D. in Language and Information Technologies from Carnegie Mellon University in 2013, along with a B.Tech and M.S. in Computer Science from IIIT Hyderabad in 2008. Office hours are from 2:30-4:30PM in 490A Cory.

Mekhail Anwar is an Adjunct Professor at UC Berkeley EECS with contact email mekhail@berkeley.edu. His research interests include Integrated Circuits (INC), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), and Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems (MEMS).

Murat Arcak is a Professor at UC Berkeley EECS, located in 569 Cory Hall, with contact email arcak@berkeley.edu. His research interests include Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Cyber-Physical Systems and Design Automation (CPSDA), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), and Power and Energy (ENE). He earned his Ph.D. and M.S. in Electrical Engineering from UC Santa Barbara in 2000 and 1997, and a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Bogazici University, Istanbul in 1996.

Ana Claudia Arias is a Professor and EE Division Chair at UC Berkeley EECS, located in 501 Cory Hall and 225 Cory Hall, with contact email acarias@eecs.berkeley.edu and phone 510-642-1728. Her research interests include Physical Electronics (PHY) and Power and Energy (ENE). She earned her Ph.D. in Physics from the University of Cambridge, UK in 2001, and M.S. and B.S. in Physics from Federal University of Paraná, Brazil in 1997 and 1995. Office hours are by appointment in 508 Cory, with assistant Alex Sandoval.

Krste Asanović is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley EECS, located in 579B Soda Hall, with contact email krste@berkeley.edu and phone 510-642-6506. His research interests include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), Integrated Circuits (INC), Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), and Design, Modeling and Analysis (DMA). He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 1998 and a B.A. in Electrical and Information Sciences from the University of Cambridge, UK in 1987. Office hours are by email appointment, with assistants Tammy Johnson and Ria Melendres Briggs.

Babak Ayazifar is a Teaching Professor at UC Berkeley EECS, located in 517 Cory Hall, with contact email ayazifar@berkeley.edu and phone 510-642-9945. His research interests include Education (EDUC) and Signal Processing (SP). He earned his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from MIT in 2003 and a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Caltech in 1989.
